root_text.an4.186:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.186:1.2: “kena nu kho, bhante, loko nīyati, kena loko parikassati, kassa ca uppannassa vasaṁ gacchatī”ti?
root_text.an4.186:2.2: Bhaddako kho te, bhikkhu, ummaggo, bhaddakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ, kalyāṇī paripucchā.
root_text.an4.186:2.4: ‘kena nu kho, bhante, loko nīyati, kena loko parikassati, kassa ca uppannassa vasaṁ gacchatī’”ti?
root_text.an4.186:2.6: “Cittena kho, bhikkhu, loko nīyati, cittena parikassati, cittassa uppannassa vasaṁ gacchatī”ti.
root_text.an4.186:3.1: “Sādhu, bhante”ti kho so bhikkhu bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā bhagavantaṁ uttari pañhaṁ apucchi:
root_text.an4.186:3.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, bahussuto dhammadharo hotī”ti?
root_text.an4.186:4.2: Bhaddako kho te, bhikkhu ummaggo, bhaddakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ, kalyāṇī paripucchā.
root_text.an4.186:4.5: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, bahussuto dhammadharo hotī’”ti?
root_text.an4.186:4.8: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthā, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.186:4.9: Catuppadāya cepi, bhikkhu, gāthāya atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti bahussuto dhammadharoti alaṁvacanāyā”ti.
root_text.an4.186:5.1: “Sādhu, bhante”ti kho so bhikkhu bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā bhagavantaṁ uttari pañhaṁ apucchi:
root_text.an4.186:5.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, sutavā nibbedhikapañño hotī”ti?
root_text.an4.186:6.2: Bhaddako kho te, bhikkhu, ummaggo, bhaddakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ, kalyāṇī paripucchā.
root_text.an4.186:6.5: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, sutavā nibbedhikapañño hotī’”ti?
root_text.an4.186:6.7: “Idha, bhikkhu, bhikkhuno ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti sutaṁ hoti, paññāya cassa atthaṁ ativijjha passati;
root_text.an4.186:6.8: ‘ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti sutaṁ hoti, paññāya cassa atthaṁ ativijjha passati;
root_text.an4.186:6.9: ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho’ti sutaṁ hoti, paññāya cassa atthaṁ ativijjha passati;
root_text.an4.186:6.10: ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti sutaṁ hoti, paññāya cassa atthaṁ ativijjha passati.
root_text.an4.186:6.11: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhu, sutavā nibbedhikapañño hotī”ti.
root_text.an4.186:7.1: “Sādhu, bhante”ti kho so bhikkhu bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā bhagavantaṁ uttari pañhaṁ apucchi:
root_text.an4.186:7.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, paṇḍito mahāpañño hotī”ti?
root_text.an4.186:8.2: Bhaddako kho te, bhikkhu, ummaggo, bhaddakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ, kalyāṇī paripucchā.
root_text.an4.186:8.5: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, paṇḍito mahāpañño hotī’”ti?
root_text.an4.186:8.7: “Idha, bhikkhu, paṇḍito mahāpañño nevattabyābādhāya ceteti na parabyābādhāya ceteti na ubhayabyābādhāya ceteti attahitaparahitaubhayahitasabbalokahitameva cintayamāno cinteti.
root_text.an4.186:8.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhu, paṇḍito mahāpañño hotī”ti.

translation_text.an4.186:1.1: Then one of the mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an4.186:1.2: “Sir, what leads the world on? What drags it around? What arises and takes control?”
translation_text.an4.186:2.2: Your approach and articulation are excellent, and it’s a good question.
translation_text.an4.186:2.4: ‘What leads the world on? What drags it around? What arises and takes control?’”
translation_text.an4.186:2.6: “Mendicant, the mind leads the world on. The mind drags it around. When the mind arises, it takes control.”
translation_text.an4.186:3.1: Saying “Good, sir”, that mendicant approved and agreed with what the Buddha said. Then he asked another question:
translation_text.an4.186:3.2: “Sir, they speak of ‘a learned memorizer of the teaching’.
translation_text.an4.186:3.3: How is a learned memorizer of the teaching defined?”
translation_text.an4.186:4.2: Your approach and articulation are excellent, and it’s a good question. …
translation_text.an4.186:4.8: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an4.186:4.9: But if anyone understands the meaning and the text of even a four-line verse, and if they practice in line with that teaching, they’re qualified to be called a ‘learned memorizer of the teaching’.”
translation_text.an4.186:5.1: Saying “Good, sir”, that mendicant approved and agreed with what the Buddha said. Then he asked another question:
translation_text.an4.186:5.2: “Sir, they speak of ‘a learned person with penetrating wisdom’.
translation_text.an4.186:5.3: How is a learned person with penetrating wisdom defined?”
translation_text.an4.186:6.2: Your approach and articulation are excellent, and it’s a good question. …
translation_text.an4.186:6.7: Take a mendicant who has heard: ‘This is suffering.’ They see what it means with penetrating wisdom.
translation_text.an4.186:6.8: They’ve heard: ‘This is the origin of suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.186:6.10: ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.’ They see what it means with penetrating wisdom.
translation_text.an4.186:6.11: That’s how an individual is learned, with penetrating wisdom.”
translation_text.an4.186:7.1: Saying “Good, sir”, that mendicant approved and agreed with what the Buddha said. Then he asked another question:
translation_text.an4.186:7.2: “Sir, they speak of ‘an astute person with great wisdom’.
translation_text.an4.186:7.3: How is an astute person with great wisdom defined?”
translation_text.an4.186:8.2: Your approach and articulation are excellent, and it’s a good question. …
translation_text.an4.186:8.7: An astute person with great wisdom is one who has no intention to hurt themselves, or to hurt others, or to hurt both. When they think, they only think of the benefit for themselves, for others, for both, and for the whole world.
translation_text.an4.186:8.8: That’s how an individual is astute, with great wisdom.”

variant_text.an4.186:4.7: Bahū → bahu (pts1ed, mr) | dhammā desitā → dhammo desito (mr)
